concepts/task-api-endpoint-design.md: Designing REST endpoints for predictable, consistent APIs through validation, error handling, authentication, pagination, versioning, and status code conventions.
concepts/task-authentication-patterns.md: Authentication patterns—session cookies, JWT tokens, OAuth2 delegation, password hashing, CSRF protection—and their security, complexity, and scalability tradeoffs for different application architectures.
concepts/task-ci-cd-pipeline.md: Automated build, test, and deployment pipeline using GitHub Actions with matrix testing, dependency caching, security scanning, and quality gates to catch bugs early and deploy verified code continuously.
concepts/task-code-migration.md: Planning code migrations across Python versions, frameworks, and libraries by identifying scope, risk, and incremental strategies for large-scale codebase upgrades.
concepts/task-configuration-setup.md: Establishes configuration hierarchy patterns for environment variables, config files, and secrets management with validation rules following 12-factor app principles.
concepts/task-database-migrations.md: Database migrations enable versioned, repeatable schema changes — additive and destructive alterations, data transformations, constraint additions — with Alembic rollback support, zero-downtime deployment strategies, and safety principles for production schema evolution.
concepts/task-debugging-sessions.md: Systematic debugging strategies including breakpoints, tracing, binary search, profiling, and profiling to isolate bugs, understand code flow, and debug performance issues without guessing.
concepts/task-dependency-management.md: Evaluation framework for adding or updating dependencies — security vulnerabilities, version compatibility, license compliance, dependency pinning, and maintenance risk assessment.
concepts/task-error-handling-design.md: Architectural patterns for deciding when to catch, propagate, transform, or chain exceptions; exception hierarchy design; logging strategies; and error recovery techniques to prevent silent failures and preserve debuggability across layer boundaries.
concepts/task-git-workflow.md: Branch strategies including trunk-based development, feature branching, and gitflow; merge conflict resolution, rebase, cherry-pick, stash, reset, and commit recovery techniques.
concepts/task-logging-observability.md: Structured logging setup with log levels, correlation IDs for distributed tracing, PII redaction, log rotation, and observability best practices for production services and request tracking.
concepts/task-package-publishing.md: Workflow for publishing Python packages to PyPI: version bumping, clean builds, TestPyPI staging, production release, and verification; includes semantic versioning and README rendering best practices.
concepts/tool-attune-hub.md: Socratic entry point for discovering and routing to the right Attune skill based on your goal—describe what you need in plain English, answer clarifying questions, and get routed to the matching skill without memorizing menus or syntax.
concepts/tool-bug-predict.md: Predicts production-bug likelihood by analyzing cyclomatic complexity, change-frequency hotspots, and anti-patterns like dangerous eval, bare exceptions, and unfinished code paths—enabling risk-focused code review and pre-merge defect prevention.
concepts/tool-coach.md: Interactive help system that explains any Attune topic through progressive depth—concept overview, step-by-step task, then full reference—without leaving your conversation to look things up.
concepts/tool-code-quality.md: Unified code quality analysis that reviews style, correctness, likely bugs, and structural health in a single pass—covering unused imports, naming, unreachable code, mutable defaults, race conditions, high coupling, and god classes before pull requests.
concepts/tool-doc-gen.md: Generates documentation from source code—docstrings, README sections, API references, and module overviews—keeping docs in sync with actual function signatures, type hints, and public APIs.
concepts/tool-fix-test.md: Automatically diagnoses failing tests by classifying root causes—import errors, mock mismatches, assertion drift, type errors, fixture issues, environment problems—and applies targeted fixes with retry logic up to 3 attempts.
concepts/tool-memory-and-context.md: Stores and retrieves persistent notes, project decisions, debugging patterns, and conventions across Claude sessions — enabling continuity without repetition between interactions.
concepts/tool-planning.md: Structures feature design before implementation with task breakdown, acceptance criteria, dependency mapping, risk assessment, and TDD scaffolding to prevent scope creep and identify blockers early.
concepts/tool-refactor-plan.md: Scans code for structural problems like god classes, duplication, cyclomatic complexity, and coupling hotspots, then builds a prioritized refactoring roadmap ranked by severity, effort, and impact to guide high-value cleanup work.
concepts/tool-release-prep.md: Preflight checklist verifying health, security, changelog, dependencies, and version consistency to produce a go/no-go assessment before publishing or tagging a release.
concepts/tool-security-audit.md: Scans codebase for known security vulnerabilities—eval/exec, path traversal, hardcoded secrets, SQL/command injection, SSRF, weak crypto—with OWASP classification and severity ratings.
concepts/tool-smart-test.md: Identifies untested code and generates pytest tests with parametrized edge cases, boundary values, and error paths for coverage gap analysis.
concepts/tool-spec.md: Spec-driven workflow that walks from brainstorm through plan decomposition, review, and approval to task execution with quality gates, preventing scope creep through structured phases and explicit sign-off before code changes.
concepts/tool-workflow-orchestration.md: Orchestrates multiple analysis workflows in sequence and combines security audit, code review, bug prediction, and testing results into a single unified report for comprehensive pre-release or CI gate assessment.
errors/bug-predict-dangerous-eval-flags-: Predicts bug-prone code patterns including dangerous eval, exec, subprocess calls, bare exceptions, and high-complexity functions to identify injection vectors and defect hotspots before production.

faqs/push-specific-tags-not-tags.md: Pushing specific Git tags individually instead of using the --tags flag to avoid rejecting already-existing tags.
